titleStmt.title: Letter from William Henry Thompson to the President & Fellows of the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, 8 January 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Henry Thompson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Sir William Henry Thompson (1861-1929) offering himself as a candidate for re-election to the Chair of Institutes of Medicine in the School of Physic.
The letter has been initialled by Ephraim MacDowel Cosgrave (1853-1925). Cosgrave served as president of the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland in 1914 and held the position until 1916. Aside from his dedication to medicine Cosgrave was also a member of the Royal Society of Antiquities of Ireland, treasurer of the Royal Zoological Society of Ireland and a founder and secretary of the Georgian Society of Dublin.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.placeName: School of Physiology, Trinity College Dublin, Dublin, Ireland
Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
School of Physiology
Trinity College. Dublin. 8th Jan 1916 Mr President & Gentlemen I beg to offer myself
as a candidate for re-election to the
Chair of Institutes of Medicine in the
School of Physic in Ireland. I am
Your obedt servt W.H. Thompson To
The President & Fellows of the Royal
College of Physicians of Ireland. Macd.C
10.I.16
